name: ESSAI 2026
venue: European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence, Vienna, Austria
description: The 4th European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence (ESSAI) will take place in Vienna, Austria from 6 to 10 July 2026. It is aimed at PhD students and young researchers and offers introductory and advanced AI courses, including special themes on Bilateral AI and Digital Humanism, alongside a co‑located ACM Europe Digital Humanism Summer School. The programme combines intensive lectures, parallel tutorial tracks, networking opportunities and a social reception at Vienna City Hall.

name: OpenEuroLLM Winter School
description: The 2026 Winter School on Multilinguality in LLM Development and Evaluation is held at the Skeikampen conference hotel north of Oslo, Norway. It targets NLP researchers, especially those from EuroHPC consortium countries, and offers doctoral training within the Circle U university alliance. The program features technical talks, hands‑on sessions on multilingual large language models, and excursions such as a walk‑through of EuroHPC experience reports, with free bus transfers from Oslo Airport.

name: Probabilistic AI School
description: The ProbAI School 2026 is a probabilistic AI summer school taking place in Vilnius, Lithuania. It aims to provide an inclusive educational environment featuring state‑of‑the‑art machine learning and artificial intelligence expertise. The program welcomes enthusiasts, students, academics, and industry professionals for intensive learning and networking.

name: BMVA Computer Vision Summer School
description: The 29th BMVA Computer Vision Summer School will be held at Durham University in Durham, England. It offers an intensive week of lectures and lab sessions covering a broad spectrum of computer‑vision topics, delivered by leading researchers from the UK and abroad. The programme features daily oral talks and hands‑on sessions designed for students and early‑career researchers seeking advanced training in the field.

name: Oxford Machine Learning Summer School 2026
description: OxML Summer 2026 is a program offered by the Oxford ML School, providing both in‑person and online learning. It offers three specialised tracks covering the latest AI theory and applications, taught by experts from leading academic and industry labs. Participants can customise their experience by selecting any combination of tracks to match their learning goals.

name: Robotics, Perception and Learning (RPL) Summer School
venue: KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Stockholm, Sweden
description: The RPL Summer School 2026 is a six‑day event organized by the Department for Robotics, Perception and Learning at KTH Royal Institute of Technology in Stockholm, Sweden. It brings together doctoral and postdoctoral researchers to discuss topics such as machine learning, computer vision, robotics, human‑robot interaction, planning and decision making through keynotes, industry talks, brainstorming sessions and student presentations. The program is hosted at the Happy Tammsvik resort and includes indoor and outdoor ice‑breaker activities, with all accommodation and meals sponsored for participants.

name: Machine Learning Crash Course at MALGA
description: The MLCC2026 Summer School – Machine Learning Crash Course – provides an introduction to fundamental machine‑learning concepts and algorithms. It is aimed at undergraduate and graduate students, as well as professionals, and is open to participants from any university after a selection process. The five‑day in‑person program in Genoa combines theoretical lectures with hands‑on lab sessions and includes a final test consisting of notebook work and a report.

name: UK Robotics Summer School
venue: National Robotarium, Heriot-Watt University, Edinburgh, United Kingdom
description: The UK Robotics Summer School 2026 is a five‑day event in Edinburgh that brings together master’s‑level students and industry practitioners to explore robotics. It offers expert‑led lectures, tutorials and hands‑on sessions covering bio‑inspired robotics, healthcare robotics, generative AI, safe AI and real‑world applications. Participants can network with academics and industry leaders while experiencing state‑of‑the‑art robotic technology, with meals and a special dinner included.

name: International Summer School for Advanced Studies on Biometrics, Behavior, and Vision
description: The 2026 Biometrics Summer School takes place in Alghero, Sardinia, Italy, hosted at the Hotel Dei Pini. It focuses on the impact of generative AI and large foundation models on biometric and human‑recognition technologies, covering machine learning, image understanding, signal analysis, neuroscience, robotics, forensic and digital forensics. The program is aimed at PhD students, post‑docs, researchers and professionals, offering intensive lectures by 20 international experts, hands‑on sessions, and the possibility of scholarships and a limited‑capacity, in‑person experience.

name: 1st Internationa School on the Cognitive, Ethical, and Societal Dimensions of AI - AIces
description: The AIces 2026 International School on the Cognitive, Ethical and Societal Dimensions of Artificial Intelligence takes place in Porto and Maia, Portugal from 30 March to 2 April 2026. It offers parallel courses, a symposium and open debates covering AI cognition, ethics and societal impact, aimed at graduate students, post‑graduates and industry practitioners. The event combines face‑to‑face interaction with live online participation and provides certificates that may be used for ECTS recognition.

name: Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal (ELS) aspects of AI and AS at Umeå University
description: The Winter School focuses on the ethical, legal, and societal aspects of Artificial Intelligence and Autonomous Systems. It offers a mix of lectures, hands‑on multidisciplinary training, a poster session, and a social program to foster networking and professional exchange. The event is aimed at students, researchers, and professionals interested in responsible AI and AS, and takes place at Umeå University in Sweden.

name: Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia (SoRAIM 2026)
description: The Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia (SoRAIM) 2026 will be held at the Escandille in Autrans, France. It aims to bring together researchers from fields such as computer vision, speech/audio processing, and human‑robot interaction to foster cross‑disciplinary exchange through courses and informal interactions. The program features expert talks on topics like affective signal processing, conversational speech synthesis, and multimodal dialogue, as well as a hands‑on session on human‑robot interaction.

name: Northern Lights Deep Learning Winter School
venue: UiT The Arctic University of Norway, Tromsø, Norway
description: The 2027 Northern Lights Deep Learning Conference includes a Winter School held in Tromsø, Norway. It runs from January 11th to 15th and focuses on deep learning topics such as image classification, object detection, segmentation, time series prediction, and speech recognition. The school brings together national and international researchers for hands‑on learning and networking, hosted by UiT The Arctic University of Norway.

name: Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS)
description: The Sixth Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS) is an 11‑day intensive program offering AI lectures, hands‑on labs, and project work. It targets senior undergraduates, graduate students, PhD candidates, faculty members, and industry professionals from Nepal and abroad. The school aims to disseminate cutting‑edge AI research, foster collaborations, and democratize AI education in South Asia.

name: EEML Summer School
venue: Eastern European Machine Learning Summer School, Cetinje, Montenegro
description: The Eastern European Machine Learning Summer School takes place in Cetinje, Montenegro from 27 July to 1 August 2026. The program features lectures and tutorials by leading researchers from institutions such as Google DeepMind, MIT, University of Oxford, and many others, covering a wide range of machine‑learning and AI topics. Although the application deadline has passed, the school offers valuable networking opportunities with academia and industry partners across Europe.

name: Mediterranean Summer School
description: The Mediterranean Machine Learning (M2L) Summer School is a five‑day program offering keynotes, lectures, and hands‑on sessions led by international AI experts. It targets doctoral and outstanding master students, as well as academics and practitioners, focusing on topics such as computer vision, NLP, generative models, and reinforcement learning. The 2027 edition will be held in Spain in September, providing networking opportunities, poster presentations, and a commitment to diversity and inclusion.

name: Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI
venue: Institut National des Postes et Télécommunications, Rabat, Morocco
description: The AI Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI is a five‑day program held at the Institut National des Postes et Télécommunications in Rabat, Morocco. It targets master, Ph.D., post‑doc and early‑career researchers, offering lectures, hands‑on labs, and project work on state‑of‑the‑art multimodal and generative AI techniques. Participants also benefit from networking opportunities with AI leaders and industry partners, fostering the next generation of AI talent in Morocco.

name: AthNLP Summer School 2026
description: The Athens Natural Language Processing Summer School 2026 (AthNLP) will be held at the NCSR Demokritos campus in Athens, Greece. The program, organized by a consortium of research centers and universities, offers intensive training on state‑of‑the‑art NLP methods for students and researchers. It also coincides with the 14th EETN Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Chania, providing additional networking opportunities.

name: Advanced Artificial Intelligence: GenAI & Deep Unsupervised Learning
venue: Lady Margaret Hall, Oxford, United Kingdom
description: The Advanced Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning summer school focuses on generative AI and deep unsupervised learning techniques. It offers a blend of theoretical lectures and practical, real‑world applications for STEM students with intermediate AI/ML experience, covering topics such as GANs, self‑supervised learning, and energy‑based models. The intensive residential or online format runs from 29 June to 17 July 2026 at Lady Margaret Hall, Oxford.

name: AI4Science Summer School
description: The 2nd Summer School on Advancing Scientific Discovery with AI brings together researchers, PhD students and post‑doctoral scholars to explore how artificial intelligence can accelerate scientific research. It covers topics such as machine learning for data analysis, AI‑driven experimental design, generative models, and the integration of AI with physical simulations, featuring hands‑on workshops using Python libraries like NumPy and PyTorch. The program aims to equip participants with practical skills and interdisciplinary insights to apply AI techniques to their own scientific investigations.

name: LUT Summer School
description: LUT Summer School is a one‑week academic programme held at the Lappeenranta campus of LUT University in Finland. It offers intensive, credit‑bearing courses that combine sustainability, climate action, marketing, management and inventive thinking across business and technology disciplines. The programme targets bachelor’s and master’s students and includes workshops, group work and a social programme set against the Finnish summer landscape.
